scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test that the function ignores non-numeric and empty lines in the m/z input string, processing only valid numeric m/z values, and returns the same count as with only valid lines.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Prove that the solution gracefully handles malformed input lines (non-numeric) without crashing and only counts valid numeric m/z values.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function should ignore non-numeric lines and empty lines, processing only valid floating point numbers.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior that naively passes all lines to the API would likely cause server error or incorrect counts.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
num_matches = _get_num_matches(db, mz_values, adducts, tolerance)
print(f'Number of unique m/z matches: {num_matches}')
result = num_matches
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new requirement introduced by this operator.
The solution must be robust to malformed input lines.
